title: Warden of the Old Guard
description: The party meets the Warden in the deepest chamber of the Whispering Cave. The first Masterite encounter.

lines: The deepest chamber was almost perfectly circular. The stone floor worn smooth not by traffic but by the slow passage of time.
The air here was different. Drier. Stiller. As if the ordinary dampness of the mountain had decided to leave this room alone.
In the center stood a figure in full plate armor, utterly still. The armor was old — not decayed, not corrupted, but old in the way things are old when they have been maintained for centuries.

steps.text: The figure's head rose. Not with aggression. With attention.

lines.speaker: Warden
lines.text: Hm. Four blades. A healer. A mage. Rearguard position on the light-fingered one. Standard formation for an irregular unit.

lines.speaker: Warden
lines.text: You're exactly what I said. Conscripts, most of you. Not chosen. Not trained for this. Which means you're here because no one with better options came.

lines.speaker: Warden
lines.text: That makes you either very brave or very unlucky.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: We've had about forty percent of each, so far.

lines.speaker: Warden
lines.text: This pass has been held for three hundred years. By my count, you are the thirty-eighth party to attempt it. The other thirty-seven turned back.

lines.speaker: Warden
lines.text: I am not telling you this to frighten you. I am telling you so the record is clear.

lines.speaker: Warden
lines.text: I have guarded this chamber since before memory. Since before your village was a plan in anyone's mind.

lines.speaker: Warden
lines.text: You will be tested. But first — there is food in the guardhouse. You may rest before we begin, if you wish.

lines.speaker: Warden
lines.text: Show me what you are, and I will show you what I have left.

lines.speaker: Rogue
lines.text: Is there a way to avoid—

steps.text: The Warden drew his sword. Not quickly. Not with aggression. The way a craftsman picks up their tool. Natural. Inevitable.

lines.speaker: Warden
lines.text: I want you to know that I bear you no ill will. This is not personal. This is purpose.
